The shaman's boundary ritual yields zero net energy because the complex exponential function remains analytic everywhere within the circular domain, ensuring the contour integral vanishes per Cauchy's theorem. This mathematical silence suggests a perfect spiritual equilibrium where no force leaks outward, a concept an engineer might call a closed system of infinite potential. The unique result implies the sacred circle acts as a self-contained vessel, holding all vibrational power internally without external exchange.

Calculating the average internal energy density requires integrating the real component of the exponential function across the polar area, yielding a value dependent on the radius squared that grows rapidly with distance. Such a calculation reveals how spiritual intensity concentrates toward the periphery, a phenomenon any engineer would study to understand structural stress or energy distribution in sacred architecture. This connection between mathematical growth and mystical power highlights the profound link between abstract analysis and ancient belief systems, showing how numbers can map the unseen forces guiding the shaman's dance.

P.S. The engineer observes that the engineer sees the connection.
